results.title: Google Releases New Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite ...
results.content:
Blog
 A.I.
 Google Gemini
 Post

# Google Releases New Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber Models

 Laurent Giret
 Jul 21, 2026
 2

Google is making three new Gemini AI models available today to make up for the delayed release of its Gemini 3.5 Pro model, which was announced at its I/O conference in May. The headliner is the Gemini 3.6 Flash model, but there’s also the fastest and more cost-effective 3.5 Flash-Lite and the cybersecurity-focused 3.5 Flash Cyber.

According to Google, Gemini 3.6 Flash is more efficient and cheaper to use than 3.5 Flash for agentic tasks. The new model also comes with additional safeguards in the domains of Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) and cyber offense misuses. [...] Lastly, Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is Google’s response to the latest flagship cybersecurity models from rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic. Google said that it can find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities at “a lower price per token than larger models.”

At the moment, access to Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is restricted to governments and trusted partners via a limited-access pilot program. Gemini 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite, however, are rolling out to the Gemini app, Google Search,  Google AI Studio, Android Studio, and the Gemini Enterprise app.

### Tagged with

 Google
 Google Gemini

### Share post [...] “3.6 Flash not only delivers a step up in coding and knowledge work, but it does this while meaningfully improving token efficiency,” the company said. “It also takes fewer reasoning steps and tool calls to accomplish multi-step workflows.”

Google’s new Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite model is optimized for agentic workflows, and it’s now the fastest model in the 3.5 series of Gemini models. It’s also a cost-efficient model with “a strong price-to-performance ratio for developers and customers running high throughput production traffic,” the company explained.

results.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Using Gemini 3.6 Flash (+ Review ...
results.content:
On July 21, 2026, Google released three models: Gemini 3.6 Flash — the updated core model, 3.5 Flash-Lite — the fastest in the series, and 3.5 Flash Cyber — a closed cybersecurity model.
 3.6 Flash improved coding, agentic, and multimodal tasks: 49% on DeepSWE versus 37% for its predecessor, and 83% on the OSWorld-Verified computer control benchmark. The knowledge base was updated from January 2025 to March 2026.
 The model has become more efficient: it uses 17% fewer output tokens, and the API generation price has dropped from $9 to $7.5 per 1 million tokens.
 3.6 Flash is already available in the Gemini app via the model dropdown, as well as in the Gemini API, AI Studio, Android Studio, Antigravity, and Gemini Enterprise. [...] On July 21, 2026, Google unveiled three new AI models at once: Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber. The first replaces 3.5 Flash as the ecosystem’s main “workhorse,” the second is the fastest in the lineup, and the third is a specialized model for finding and fixing vulnerabilities in code, available only through a closed program.

> We’re rolling out three new models to make AI agents faster, smarter, and cheaper at scale:  
>   
> 🔵 Gemini 3.6 Flash: It uses fewer tokens than 3.5 Flash to deliver higher quality work at the exact same cost.  
>   
> 🔵 Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite: A fast, cost-effective option for everyday tasks… pic.twitter.com/SP1eU3FCW5
>
> — Google DeepMind (@GoogleDeepMind) July 21, 2026 [...] — 83% versus 78.4%, and in office tasks GDPval-AA v2 — 1,421 points versus 1,349. The knowledge base has been updated through March 2026 — the previous version ended in January 2025.

A separate focus is efficiency. Google has taken developers’ complaints about models being “too verbose” into account: 3.6 Flash uses 17% fewer output tokens for the same tasks and requires fewer intermediate steps in multi-stage workflows. API pricing has also dropped: $1.5 per 1 million input tokens and $7.5 per 1 million output tokens — versus $9 for generation with 3.5 Flash.

The model is already available in the Gemini API, Google AI Studio, Android Studio, the Antigravity agent environment, Gemini Enterprise, and the consumer Gemini app — both on the web and on smartphones.

results.title: Google Releases Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite ... - MarkTechPost
results.content:
## Community Reaction

Reaction split along predictable lines. Builders welcomed the price and efficiency. The delayed flagship drew the loudest criticism. On Hacker News, some argued Google is over-selling capacity it cannot reliably provision, citing frustrating hands-on coding sessions. The gated Flash Cyber release opened a dual-use debate about who should hold automated exploit-finding tools.

The dashboard below aggregates that discussion by platform. It is a qualitative editorial synthesis, not a scraped dataset, and the method note is embedded.

## Availability [...] The internal evaluations are striking. On Google’s Big Sleep evaluation, Flash Cyber significantly surpassed mainline 3.5 Flash and 3.6 Flash. On the V8 JavaScript engine, it found 55 unique confirmed issues at a fixed number of invocations. That compares to 47 for mainline 3.5 Flash and 36 for Claude Opus 4.6. It caught 10 issues the other two models missed. In one real-world test, Google’s Cloud Vulnerability Research team used it to find remote-code-execution flaws in public APIs within two hours.

## Community Reaction [...] # Google Releases Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber: A Cheaper, More Token-Efficient Flash Tier Built for Agentic Workloads

By

Asif Razzaq

-

Developers building production agents need higher token efficiency, lower latency, and more reliable performance. Today, Google has released three new Gemini models. The lineup is Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber. All three sit in the Flash tier, which Google tunes for speed, cost, and high-volume agentic work rather than maximum reasoning depth.

## Gemini 3.6 Flash: better quality, fewer tokens, lower price

results.title: What is Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite?
results.content:
It landed as part of a three-model Flash refresh: the new workhorse Gemini 3.6 Flash, this Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a gated security model called Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber. You can read Google’s own rundown on the Google blog and the model details on the DeepMind Flash page. Flash-Lite sits at the bottom of that stack on price and at the top on raw speed.

## Wait, why is it 3.5 and not 3.6?

Here’s the confusing part. The new workhorse model is Gemini 3.6 Flash. But Flash-Lite stayed on 3.5, and so did the gated Cyber security model. So one launch, one day, three models, two version numbers. That’s not a typo on Google’s part or a mistake in this article. Google shipped mixed versioning: the flagship Flash jumped to 3.6, while the Lite and Cyber variants kept the 3.5 label. [...] Why is it 3.5 and not 3.6? Mixed versioning. Google bumped the workhorse Flash to 3.6, but kept Flash-Lite and the gated Cyber model on the 3.5 label in the same launch. The number tracks the model line, not the launch date.

Is this the old Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite? No. Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite is a newer model. The older 3.1 Flash-Lite is the previous generation. Same family name, different model and version, so check the model id `gemini-3.5-flash-lite` to be sure you’re on the new one.

Is Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite free? There’s a free tier through Google AI Studio, and it’s rate-limited. It’s fine for testing and light use. For production volume you move to a paid API key, and Google may use free-tier data to improve its products. [...] From there you can turn that request into a saved regression test and schedule it to run so you catch a broken response or a pricing change before your users do. Apidog doesn’t run the model or replace the Gemini API; it’s the client you use to call, validate, and monitor the endpoint. Download Apidog if you want to test the `gemini-3.5-flash-lite` endpoint alongside the rest of your API stack.

button

## FAQ

Is Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite the same as Gemini 3.6 Flash? No. They’re two different models from the same July 21, 2026 refresh. Flash-Lite is the cheapest, fastest tier for simple high-volume work. 3.6 Flash is the pricier workhorse with stronger reasoning and coding.
